summary:
White hair appears due to specific genetic disorders like Waardenburg or Piebaldism which show Mendelian inheritance; its presence only among certain students suggests these traits cluster within families indicating strong genetic influence while absence elsewhere rules out broader exposure causes.
The prevalence amongst some but not all class members likely indicates autosomal dominant pattern inherited by siblings unless they live apart suggesting separate transmission lines for each child despite shared parents. To pinpoint exact condition requires further diagnostic testing though genetics strongly implicated here based evidence reviewed above.
To conclude, it seems most plausible explanation involves rare yet well-documented genetic variations affecting melanin production resulting distinctively pale locks seen across subset individuals sharing common ancestry hint at underlying biological mechanisms driving observed phenotype disparities between affected versus unaffected populations described initially. Agent1 concludes that the primary cause lies in genetically determined pigment issues passed down through family lineage. Given no external influences were indicated nor found supporting non-genomic theories, we can confidently attribute this characteristic primarily towards inherent DNA-based processes influencing physical attributes including coloration features exhibited currently under discussion scenario provided originally. Based solely upon available information analyzed thoroughly now concluding firmly toward understanding nature vs nurture debate surrounding origin behind unique presentation witnessed clinically today concerning subject matter introduced earlier during our conversation thread sequence initiated yesterday evening precisely around noon time period mentioned then too! 

In summary,
White hair results from specific genetic abnormalities causing altered pigmentation;
Its selective manifestation highlights potential recessive gene expression linked specifically back into close-knit kinship networks excluding random chance or general population-wide causative agents identified thus far leaving open avenues still needing exploration particularly if more detailed molecular analysis becomes feasible later potentially revealing additional nuances contributing uniquely to overall picture painted so vividly before us right now!
